skill.name: fal-workflow
skill.description: Generate workflow JSON files for chaining AI models.
skill.summary: Create structured workflow JSON for multi-model AI pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two hardcoded URL findings and two weak cryptography findings. Review found no executable code, no cryptographic operations, and no network request logic; the URLs are source references only. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from frontmatter text, and no prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Source URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 point to the source repository for attribution and additional information. They are not used by executable code and do not send data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only markdown and frontmatter. The URLs are static documentation links with no fetch, curl, API client, or other runtime network behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 do not show cryptographic code or algorithm selection. The lines describe the skill purpose and contain no hashing, encryption, signing, or key handling logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain descriptive text at both locations. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent workflow files when chaining multiple AI models. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured JSON plans for fal-style AI pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of workflow JSON files for chained AI model steps.
Helps define model sequence, inputs, outputs, and handoff structure.
Supports planning repeatable AI pipelines before implementation.
Provides a concise workflow pattern for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
References the upstream fal workflow skill source for context.
limitations: It does not execute workflows or call fal APIs by itself.
It does not validate generated JSON against a formal schema.
It provides general guidance, not provider-specific deployment code.
It has limited embedded documentation beyond the short skill instructions.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Media Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a first workflow outline that chains prompt generation, image generation, and post-processing steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Model Handoffs
use_cases.description: Draft consistent JSON fields for inputs, outputs, dependencies, and step names across an AI workflow.

use_cases.title: Document an AI Process
use_cases.description: Convert a planned multi-model process into a structured workflow artifact for team review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple workflow JSON plan for chaining two AI models. Include each step name, input, output, and dependency.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a workflow JSON plan for my AI pipeline and include notes about required inputs, expected outputs, and failure cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Step Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a workflow JSON plan for a four-step AI pipeline. Include model roles, data handoffs, retry guidance, and final output format.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Improve a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this workflow design for unclear dependencies, missing outputs, and brittle handoffs. Then propose a cleaner workflow JSON structure.

output_examples.input: A request to chain prompt expansion with image generation.
output_examples.output: A structured workflow outline with a prompt expansion step, an image generation step, named inputs, named outputs, and dependency notes.

output_examples.input: A request to document a multi-model content pipeline.
output: A clear sequence of model steps.
A summary of data passed between steps.
A final output description ready for team review.

output_examples.input: A request to improve an existing workflow plan.
output_examples.output: A revised workflow description that clarifies step names, required parameters, output expectations, and error handling notes.

best_practices: Define each model step with one clear purpose and one expected output.
Name dependencies explicitly so downstream steps are easy to trace.
Review generated workflow JSON before using it in production systems.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated workflow JSON as validated production configuration.
Do not mix unrelated model tasks inside a single workflow step.
Do not include secrets, tokens, or private URLs in workflow examples.

faq.question: Does this skill run fal workflows?
faq.answer: No. It helps generate workflow JSON guidance and does not execute workflows.

faq.question: Can it call external APIs?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill file contains guidance text and source links only.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it validate JSON output?
faq.answer: No. Users should validate generated workflow files with their own schema or runtime checks.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only low-risk false positives and no executable dangerous behavior.

faq.question: What should users provide in prompts?
faq.answer: Users should provide model names, step order, inputs, outputs, and any handoff requirements.
